perks.summary: WebAuthn approval (P3-T04) — destructive grants carry a doc-bound (challenge=sha256(JCS)) hardware approval, verified offline

P3-T11 tier wiring to P2 sandbox profiles — the grant's sandbox_tier selects the confinement backend (community->gVisor/runsc, core/verified->bwrap) as a MONOTONE floor over the operator --backend, enforced at the grant via exod.run_step; a perk's declared tier flows perks.json->govd->grant->catalog; community no-secrets floor holds at schema + runtime
Citrinitas publish gate (P3-T09) — verified-tier admission via alchemy extract+conserve+classify+concord; seeded triple-block + chip-wide concord
engine attestation + mutual handshake (P3-T05) — publisher-signed engine digest; 1-byte tamper on either side → engine_unattested; health matches signed release
feed availability tiers (P3-T12) — read-only proceeds to grace-2, destructive refuses, expired fails closed, recovery re-converges
revocation-in-flight (P3-T13) — boundary halt lets the in-progress step finish then refuses; critical kills immediately
key-rotation drill (P3-T06) — cross-signed record, overlap honored, post-revocation old-key grant refuses
publish-time manifest lint (P3-T10) — catches undeclared binary/egress + capability mismatch (100% of seeded defects)
governed release receipt (P3-T15) — dual-signed chip + engine with stored transparency proof; any leg tamper fails closed
finalized receipts (P3-T14) — dual Ed25519-DSSE over one in-toto statement; single/tampered rejected
signed revocation feed (P3-T03) — monotonic {seq,expires,revoked[]}; revoked refused, stale fails closed, rollback refused
publisher signing (P3-T01) — cosign over the chip manifest; tri-layer refusal (chipfetch/govd/exod) vs the pinned TUF root
TSA time anchors (P3-T07) — high-value receipts need an offline-verifiable TSA token; absence blocks settlement
offline transparency proofs (P3-T02) — Merkle log + publisher-signed tree head; inclusion proof verifies offline vs the pinned root, no live Rekor
P3-T16 SECURITY.md doorbell (M12) — the repo SECURITY.md must name a contact, an encrypted-reporting key/mechanism, and an acknowledgement SLA
